footer.tagline: Valid digital consent forms · Law 41/2002 · GDPR

compliance.title: Legally valid electronic signature
compliance.subtitle: Every consent form signed in ConsentsPro complies with the eIDAS Regulation (EU) 910/2014 on electronic identification and trust services.

biometric.title: Biometric signature of the stroke
biometric.desc: We capture every point of the stroke when signing: coordinates, exact time, and finger or pen pressure, to verify the signature is genuine.

timestamp.title: Certified timestamp
timestamp.desc: Every signature is linked to the exact date and time certified by our server, not by the signer's device.

device.title: Device fingerprint
device.desc: We record the IP address and the device or browser used to sign, as additional proof of identity.

hash.title: Document fingerprint
hash.desc: We generate a unique cryptographic hash for every signature, allowing any later alteration of the document to be detected.

audit.title: Audit trail
audit.desc: Every signature is stored in a tamper-evident audit trail, with the full history of the signing process.

doctor.title: Joint doctor-patient signature
doctor.desc: The healthcare professional signs alongside the patient, recording both parties' involvement in the same document.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before you get started

q1.q: Is the electronic signature legally valid?
q1.a: Yes. Every signed consent form complies with the eIDAS Regulation (EU) 910/2014 and applicable law, with biometric signature of the stroke, certified timestamp, device fingerprint, and document hash.

q2.q: Is a paper consent form, or one without all this eIDAS certification, still valid?
q2.a: Yes, a hand-signed paper consent form is also legally valid, since the law doesn't require the signature to be electronic. The advantage of ConsentsPro is that, in the event of a claim or inspection, it's far harder to challenge: alongside the signature, the certified date and time, the device used, and a hash proving the document hasn't been altered are all recorded automatically — something paper, or a simple electronic signature without these safeguards, can't offer.

q3.q: Do I need to install anything?
q3.a: No. ConsentsPro runs 100% in the cloud, from any browser and device — computer, tablet, or mobile — with no installation required.

q4.a: Yes, you can switch or upgrade your plan anytime directly from the platform, with no commitment.

q5.a: Yes. All patient data processing complies with GDPR, including the image authorization record for the use of photos.

q6.q: Can I use it with foreign patients?
q6.a: Yes, consent forms are available in 31 languages, with automatic translation of the templates.

q7.a: Every plan includes technical support to help you with any question about the platform.

q8.q: How long does it take to get the clinic up and running?
q8.a: The initial setup — team, treatments, and consent templates — is done in minutes from the admin panel. Configuring the AI agents depends on the complexity of each clinic, with an average timeframe of around 15 days.

q9.q: Do my patients need to create a password?
q9.a: No. They access their portal via Magic Link, a secure access link they receive by email, with no passwords to remember.

q10.q: What equipment do I need to use ConsentsPro?
q10.a: Just a computer with an internet connection. It can have a touchscreen, to sign directly there, or it can be a regular desktop or laptop without touch, paired with a clinic mobile or tablet for signing. In this second case, the consent form is automatically handed off from the computer to the touch device just by scanning a QR code once: the doctor and patient read and sign it there with a finger, and everything is recorded exactly as if it had been signed on the computer itself.
